html.message: First run with new features (pt-8 padding, overlay mode) - full visual QA performed

output.reason: New features added: 32px top padding and overlay mode
output.compositionNotes: All 11 header instances render correctly across all three browsers. The overall structure, proportions, and visual weight match expectations from cached Figma tokens. No content overflow or clipping observed.

featureValidation.evidence: Visible gap of approximately 32px above every dark nav bar across all 11 header instances at all breakpoints. The pt-8 class is applied unconditionally on the outer wrapper (HeaderMain.php line 42). Visually consistent spacing observed in all 12 screenshots.

The last header instance ('Display Above Content') clearly shows the dark nav bar floating above the paragraph text content below it. At 1440px, the lorem ipsum text is visible directly beneath the header, confirming the header overlays rather than pushes content down. The page audit confirms data-display-above-content='1' on the last instance only, and .header-main.absolute found on exactly 1 element. Implementation uses negative margin-bottom via CSS variable (-mb-[var(--header-main-height,0px)]) to pull subsequent content up, combined with absolute positioning (absolute left-0 right-0 z-200) on the nav bar.

observations: Logo spans both rows (utility + nav) on the left side - proportional, approximately 58px max height
Utility row visible on right with search field (~250px wide) + vertical separator + SEP external link
Nav items row below utility row, right-aligned with gap between items (~32px)
Dark charcoal background (#1e1e1c) with rounded corners (~25px border-radius) on the nav bar
Padding inside nav bar appears correct: ~32px horizontal (px-8), ~16px vertical (py-4)
Top padding above nav bar visually ~32px (pt-8)
Overlay instance: header overlays paragraph text below, no extra whitespace gap between header and content
Non-overlay instances: content flows normally below each header with expected spacing
Desktop layout maintained at 1024px - logo on left, utility row + nav on right
All elements properly sized and positioned
Top padding consistent with desktop
Overlay instance visible and functioning correctly
Mobile layout activates: single row with logo + hamburger toggle
SEP mobile button visible above nav bar where configured (full-width rounded pill button)
Top padding (pt-8) visible above the mobile layout
Overlay instance renders correctly in mobile layout
Compact mobile layout: logo (~26px max height) + hamburger on right
SEP mobile button correctly shown above nav bar for instances with sepLink configured
Top padding of ~32px visible above all instances
Overlay instance: header overlays content below correctly at mobile width
No text overflow or clipping issues

crossBrowserComparison.notes: All three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) render identically across all four breakpoints. No browser-specific visual regressions detected. The overlay mode, padding, rounded corners, and layout structure are consistent.

regressionCheck.notes: All 10 non-overlay header instances (Full Content, Minimal Content, Hide on Scroll Down variants, Not Fixed on Scroll, Submenu Alignment Right, Many Nav Items, Long Submenu Items, SEP Link Only) render correctly without any visible regressions. The pt-8 top padding addition does not break any existing layout patterns.
